raw_recent_text: "Ugh, not again," you spout as the old wagon shutters, then dies.
Stranded on the road again, but this time, your money has gone dry.
All but your 50 dollar bill that Grandma gave you on her last Christmas.
You've been saving it for when you needed it most, but surely, it won't be enough.
The door creaks open, and you step out into the night sky, coughing up the smoke from your fried vehicle.
After pushing your car off the road and between the trees, there isn't much else left for you to do,
so you begin to wander down the dark, lonely street.
But at the end of the road, where concrete turned to stone turned to dirt, you notice a light up ahead, on the top of a hill.
As you waltz into the old, wooden shack, your eyes begin to light up with the fire of a thousand suns.
Roulette wheels! Poker tables! And in a dark corner of the abandoned casino, sits a dealer, shuffling cards for a new round of Blackjack.
That 50 dollars might just come in handy after all. Thanks, Grandma!
As you go to sit down at the table, you hear the Dealer cough, then watch as he sits up.
In a deep, and yet strained voice, the Dealer, cloaked in darkness, poses a question to you.
Would you like to play a game of Blackjack?
You have $50

Broken nose. Broken hand. Maybe a black eye too.
As the sun begins to set, and the stars light up in the night sky, you walk to the casino, eager to play more Blackjack.
Shuffle. Deal. Win. Lose. Repeat. Shall we?
Something's definitely wrong with your body. That's for certain.
=== CAR TROUBLE ===
There's a grinding noise from your water pump. And your car is starting to overheat.
When the water pump goes completely, your engine will follow.
Water pump replacement: $300-500. Not optional.
You can't afford it. You drive with one eye on the temperature gauge.
Your wagon isn't road-ready, but there are still places you can reach before sundown.
Would you like to spend your day driving somewhere?
1. Doctor's Office
2. Stay Home
Choose a number:
You can't sleep. Your brain won't stop calculating odds.
If I bet X and win Y, then I could... no wait, that's not right...
You stare at the ceiling until dawn. Thinking. Always thinking. The math never adds up to freedom.
As the sun begins to set, and the stars light up in the night sky, you walk to the casino, eager to play more Blackjack.
You've got that look in your eye. Hope. Or madness. Sometimes they're the same.

Fantastic!
You've survived 2 days!
Yesterday, at this time, you had $37.
Since then, you've accumulated $2.
That brings you to a grand total of $39!
You're in the 'ramen noodle' tax bracket.
Good progress so far, pal. Keep it up.
I read this once in a magazine:
"If you don't want to be mistaken for a doormat, get off the damn floor."
Press a key to continue: ~ ~ ~ Morning, Day 3 ~ ~ ~  True
You wake up feeling like a million bucks. Well, maybe a few thousand bucks. But still great.
You step out of your car and nearly collide with... a mime? Full striped shirt, white face paint, the whole nine yards.
The mime stares at you. You stare back.
Without a word (obviously), the mime begins to act out a scene. He's pretending to be trapped in a box. Classic.
Then he mimes... crying? Counting money? Losing at cards?
Wait. Is he acting out YOUR life?
The mime finishes with a dramatic death scene, tongue out and everything, then springs back up and takes a bow.
Without a word (obviously), the mime begins to act out a scene. He's pretending to be trapped in a box. Classic.
Then he mimes... crying? Counting money? Losing at cards?
Wait. Is he acting out YOUR life?
The mime finishes with a dramatic death scene, tongue out and everything, then springs back up and takes a bow.
The mime looks devastated. He mimes a single tear rolling down his cheek, then slowly backs away, never breaking eye contact.
You feel kind of bad about that.
Something's definitely wrong with your body. That's for certain.
Your wagon isn't road-ready, but there are still places you can reach before sundown.
Would you like to spend your day driving somewhere?
1. Doctor's Office
2. Stay Home
Choose a number:
The dream is about water. Rising water. You're in the car and it's filling up. First your feet. Then your lap. Then your chest.
You can't open the doors. The windows are locked. The water is warm, which is somehow worse.
You gasp awake. Bone dry. Heart hammering. You open the car door just to prove you can.
Your mind feels... foggy. Something isn't right.
As the sun begins to set, and the stars light up in the night sky, you walk to the casino, eager to play more Blackjack.
You feel anxious tonight.
You look... determined. Or desperate. Hard to tell.

Something wet touches your hand through the cracked window. You panic-then see the fluffy face of a happy dog staring at you.
A German Shepherd this time, tail wagging so hard its whole body shakes. You can't help but smile.
Before you get a chance to check the dog's collar to see where it came from, the dog bolts down the road, eager to cheer up someone else. It was a good dog.
Something's definitely wrong with your body. That's for certain.

Choose a number:
You're chopping vegetables when your phone buzzes. You look away for one second...
The knife goes straight through your finger. You can see bone.
Blood is everywhere. You're going to need stitches. Probably surgery.
Deep laceration. Nearly severed your finger.
As the sun begins to set, and the stars light up in the night sky, you walk to the casino, eager to play more Blackjack.
You feel anxious tonight.
You smell like daylight and poor decisions. Sit.

You might not be winning the race, but at least you're in the running, right? Sort of?
I'll leave you with a quote:

"In the grand tapestry of existence, one must consider the intricate dance of the cosmos, where each celestial body moves in perfect harmony, except when they don't, which is most of the time, really, leading one to ponder whether the stars are just freestyling it. This brings to mind the importance of breakfast, the most important meal of a day that is itself a construct, much like the notion that socks should always match or that cats have any respect for personal space, which is to say, it's all a matter of perspective, isn't it? And speaking of perspective, have you ever noticed how small a plane looks in the sky, which is itself a vast canvas of blue, or gray, or black, depending on the time, which, as we've established, is a construct?"

His eyes are red. His hands are shaking when he scans your items. He drops your change twice.
"Sorry. Sorry about that. Long night."
He tries to smile but it comes out wrong. Like he forgot how smiles work.

Your current sanity: 69%
A bird is singing outside your car. You don't want to punch it. Progress.
You drive your car to a gas station for supplies. Just snacks. Maybe some coffee.
You're browsing the chips when the door slams open.
"EVERYBODY ON THE GROUND! NOW!"
A man with a shotgun. Ski mask. Shaking hands. This is really happening.
The cashier freezes. A mother clutches her child. An old man drops his coffee.
The robber swings the gun around, wild-eyed.
Would you like to spend your day driving somewhere?
1. Doctor's Office
2. Stay Home
Choose a number:
Sitting in your car, your mind drifts. You dream of water. Dark water. Deep water. Rising water.
You're in a car—your car—and water is pouring in through every crack.
You can't open the doors. The pressure is too much. The windows won't break.
The water reaches your waist. Your chest. Your neck. Your mouth.
You scream but water fills your lungs instead of air.
You wake up GASPING. Clawing at your throat. Soaking wet with sweat.
Just a dream. Just a dream. Just a—
Your feet are wet. You look down.
Rain. It's raining. Water leaked through a crack in your window.
Just rain. You're fine. You're fine. You're fine.
The edges of your vision blur. Reality feels thin.
As the sun begins to set, and the stars light up in the night sky, you walk to the casino, eager to play more Blackjack.
The casino lights seem too bright. Too hungry. They're watching you.
Take your seat. The wheel of fortune turns for no one.
